paracetamol.uses: Fever, headache, mild to moderate pain, body ache
paracetamol.general_guidance: Take with water. Standard adult dose 500-650mg every 4-6 hours. Do not combine with other paracetamol products. Avoid alcohol.
paracetamol.contraindications: Liver disease, alcoholism, severe kidney disease

ibuprofen.uses: Pain, inflammation, fever, menstrual cramps, arthritis, sports injuries
ibuprofen.general_guidance: Always take with food or milk. Avoid on empty stomach. Do not take for more than 5 days without doctor advice.
ibuprofen.contraindications: Stomach ulcers, kidney disease, pregnancy (3rd trimester), heart disease, aspirin allergy

cetirizine.uses: Allergic rhinitis, urticaria, hay fever, skin allergies, watery eyes
cetirizine.general_guidance: Take at bedtime as it may cause mild drowsiness. Less sedating than older antihistamines.

levocetrizine.uses: Allergic rhinitis, urticaria, perennial allergies — stronger and less sedating than Cetirizine
levocetrizine.general_guidance: Take in the evening. Most effective long-acting antihistamine.

fexofenadine.uses: Allergic rhinitis, seasonal allergies, hives — non-drowsy option
fexofenadine.general_guidance: Does NOT cause drowsiness. Ideal for daytime use. Do not take with grapefruit juice.

omeprazole.uses: Acidity, GERD, stomach ulcers, heartburn, H. pylori (with antibiotics)
omeprazole.general_guidance: Take 30 minutes before breakfast. Do not crush or chew capsules. Use for minimum effective duration.
omeprazole.contraindications: Liver disease (use cautiously), osteoporosis risk with long-term use

pantoprazole.uses: Acidity, GERD, gastritis, peptic ulcers, Zollinger-Ellison syndrome
pantoprazole.general_guidance: Take before meals. Swallow whole — do not crush. Often preferred over Omeprazole for fewer drug interactions.

rabeprazole.general_guidance: Take before meals. Works faster than Omeprazole.

azithromycin.uses: Respiratory infections, skin infections, ear infections, typhoid, STIs
azithromycin.general_guidance: Complete the full course. Can take with or without food. Single-dose regimens available for some conditions.
azithromycin.contraindications: Liver disease, heart rhythm problems (QT prolongation), myasthenia gravis

amoxicillin.uses: Throat infections, ear infections, UTI, skin infections, dental infections, H. pylori
amoxicillin.general_guidance: Complete full antibiotic course. Take with food to reduce stomach upset.

amoxicillin_clavulanate.uses: Sinusitis, tonsillitis, chest infections, UTI, skin infections, dental abscesses — stronger than plain amoxicillin
amoxicillin_clavulanate.general_guidance: Take with meals. Complete the full course. More effective against resistant bacteria.

ciprofloxacin.uses: UTI, typhoid, traveller's diarrhea, respiratory infections, skin infections, bone infections
ciprofloxacin.general_guidance: Take on empty stomach or with food. Drink plenty of water. Avoid dairy products and antacids 2 hours before/after.
ciprofloxacin.contraindications: Children under 18 (except specific cases), pregnancy, tendon problems

metronidazole.uses: Amoebic dysentery, giardiasis, bacterial vaginosis, dental infections, H. pylori, anaerobic infections
metronidazole.general_guidance: AVOID alcohol completely during treatment and 48 hours after. Take with food.
metronidazole.contraindications: Alcohol consumption, first trimester pregnancy, blood disorders

doxycycline.uses: Acne, malaria prophylaxis, respiratory infections, STIs, Lyme disease, typhus
doxycycline.general_guidance: Take with full glass of water. Sit upright for 30 minutes after. Avoid lying down. Take with food to reduce nausea. Avoid sun exposure.
doxycycline.contraindications: Children under 8, pregnancy, breastfeeding, severe liver disease

metformin.general_guidance: Take with meals. Start with low dose and increase gradually. Monitor blood sugar regularly. Do not skip doses.
metformin.contraindications: Kidney disease (eGFR <30), liver disease, heart failure, alcohol abuse

glimepiride.general_guidance: Take with breakfast. Monitor blood sugar — risk of hypoglycemia. Carry glucose sweets.

atorvastatin.uses: High cholesterol (hyperlipidemia), prevention of heart attack and stroke
atorvastatin.general_guidance: Can be taken at any time of day. Report unexplained muscle pain or weakness immediately.
atorvastatin.contraindications: Liver disease, pregnancy, breastfeeding, unexplained muscle pain

rosuvastatin.uses: High cholesterol — more potent than atorvastatin at equivalent doses
rosuvastatin.general_guidance: Take at any time. Report muscle pain. Avoid excessive alcohol.
rosuvastatin.contraindications: Liver disease, pregnancy, kidney disease (high doses)

diclofenac.uses: Pain, arthritis, muscle pain, menstrual pain, post-surgical pain, inflammation
diclofenac.general_guidance: Take with food. Available as oral, topical gel, and injection. Gel form has fewer systemic side effects.
diclofenac.contraindications: Stomach ulcers, kidney disease, heart disease, last trimester pregnancy

naproxen.uses: Arthritis, menstrual cramps, tendinitis, gout, back pain — longer-acting than ibuprofen
naproxen.general_guidance: Take with food. Twice daily dosing due to longer half-life.
naproxen.contraindications: Stomach ulcers, kidney disease, heart disease

ondansetron.uses: Nausea and vomiting from chemotherapy, surgery, gastroenteritis, pregnancy
ondansetron.general_guidance: Can be taken with or without food. ODT (orally dissolving tablet) form available. Very effective for vomiting.

domperidone.uses: Nausea, vomiting, bloating, slow gastric emptying, GERD with regurgitation
domperidone.general_guidance: Take 15-30 minutes before meals. Do not use for more than 7 days without medical advice.
domperidone.contraindications: Heart disease (QT prolongation risk), prolactinoma, bleeding bowel conditions

loperamide.uses: Acute and chronic diarrhea, traveller's diarrhea, IBS-D
loperamide.general_guidance: Take after each loose stool. Do not exceed 8mg/day. Do not use if blood in stool or high fever.
loperamide.contraindications: Bacterial diarrhea with fever, blood in stool, children under 2, UC

ranitidine.uses: Acidity, GERD, stomach ulcers, Zollinger-Ellison syndrome
ranitidine.general_guidance: Take 30 minutes before meals or at bedtime. Avoid alcohol.

montelukast.uses: Asthma prevention, allergic rhinitis, exercise-induced bronchospasm
montelukast.general_guidance: Take in the evening. NOT for acute asthma attacks. Monitor for mood/behavioral changes.

salbutamol.uses: Acute asthma attack, bronchospasm, COPD — rescue inhaler
salbutamol.general_guidance: Shake inhaler before use. Breathe in slowly. Hold breath 10 seconds after. Rinse mouth after use. For acute attacks only.

aspirin.uses: Pain, fever (low dose), heart attack prevention, stroke prevention, blood clot prevention
aspirin.general_guidance: Low-dose (75mg): take once daily after food for heart protection. High dose: for pain/fever. Avoid in children under 16 (Reye's syndrome).
aspirin.contraindications: Bleeding disorders, stomach ulcers, children under 16, pregnancy, dengue fever, asthma (aspirin-sensitive)

clopidogrel.uses: Prevention of heart attack and stroke, after coronary stent placement, ACS
clopidogrel.general_guidance: Take with or without food. Do not stop without doctor advice. Tell all doctors you take this before any procedure.
clopidogrel.contraindications: Active bleeding, peptic ulcer, severe liver disease

amlodipine.general_guidance: Take at same time daily. May cause ankle swelling and flushing initially.

enalapril.uses: Hypertension, heart failure, diabetic kidney disease
enalapril.general_guidance: May cause dry cough in some patients (switch to ARB if bothersome). Check potassium levels.
enalapril.contraindications: Pregnancy, bilateral renal artery stenosis, history of ACE-inhibitor angioedema

losartan.uses: Hypertension, heart failure, diabetic nephropathy, stroke prevention
losartan.general_guidance: Does NOT cause cough (unlike ACE inhibitors). Take with or without food. Monitor kidney function and potassium.
losartan.contraindications: Pregnancy, bilateral renal artery stenosis, hyperkalemia

telmisartan.general_guidance: Long-acting — once daily. Take with or without food. Good choice for patients who develop cough with ACE inhibitors.
telmisartan.contraindications: Pregnancy, liver disease, bile duct obstruction

propranolol.uses: Hypertension, angina, arrhythmia, anxiety, tremor, migraine prevention, hyperthyroidism
propranolol.general_guidance: Do NOT stop abruptly — taper dose. Take at same time daily.
propranolol.contraindications: Asthma, COPD, severe bradycardia, uncontrolled heart failure

metoprolol.uses: Hypertension, heart failure, angina, arrhythmia, heart attack prevention
metoprolol.general_guidance: Take with food. Do NOT stop abruptly. Safer than propranolol in lung disease.

warfarin.uses: DVT, pulmonary embolism, atrial fibrillation, mechanical heart valves
warfarin.general_guidance: Regular INR blood tests required. Very sensitive to food (especially vitamin K in greens) and other medicines. MANY drug interactions.
warfarin.contraindications: Active bleeding, pregnancy, severe liver disease, poor compliance

insulin.uses: Type 1 Diabetes, Type 2 Diabetes uncontrolled on oral meds, Diabetic Ketoacidosis
insulin.general_guidance: Must be administered by injection. Store in refrigerator. Never freeze. Rotate injection sites.

levothyroxine.general_guidance: Take on empty stomach 30-60 minutes before breakfast. No calcium, iron, or antacids within 4 hours. Regular TSH testing required.

prednisolone.uses: Asthma, allergic reactions, autoimmune diseases, inflammatory conditions, skin diseases
prednisolone.general_guidance: Take with food. Do NOT stop abruptly after long-term use — taper dose. Take in the morning to minimize adrenal suppression.
prednisolone.contraindications: Active infections (untreated), peptic ulcers, diabetes (worsens control), osteoporosis

hydroxychloroquine.general_guidance: Take with food or milk. Eye examination required every year during long-term use (retinal toxicity risk).

alprazolam.uses: Anxiety disorders, panic disorder, short-term anxiety relief
alprazolam.general_guidance: PRESCRIPTION ONLY. Risk of dependence — use minimum effective dose for shortest duration. Do NOT stop abruptly. Avoid alcohol.
alprazolam.contraindications: Respiratory disease, sleep apnea, liver disease, pregnancy, alcohol dependence

escitalopram.uses: Depression, generalised anxiety disorder, panic disorder, OCD
escitalopram.general_guidance: Takes 2-4 weeks for full effect. Do NOT stop abruptly. Take at same time daily.
escitalopram.contraindications: MAO inhibitor use within 14 days, long QT syndrome

sertraline.uses: Depression, OCD, PTSD, panic disorder, social anxiety, PMDD
sertraline.general_guidance: Start low and titrate. Takes 4-6 weeks for full effect. Take with food.

clonazepam.uses: Epilepsy, panic disorder, restless leg syndrome, anxiety
clonazepam.general_guidance: PRESCRIPTION ONLY. Dependence risk. Do not stop abruptly — may trigger seizures.

pregabalin.uses: Neuropathic pain (diabetic, post-herpetic), fibromyalgia, epilepsy, generalized anxiety
pregabalin.general_guidance: May cause dizziness and drowsiness. Do not drive initially. Do not stop abruptly.

gabapentin.uses: Neuropathic pain, epilepsy, postherpetic neuralgia, restless legs syndrome
gabapentin.general_guidance: Start low, increase gradually. May cause drowsiness. Avoid abrupt stoppage.

tamsulosin.uses: Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia (BPH) — improves urine flow, kidney stone passage
tamsulosin.general_guidance: Take 30 minutes after same meal daily. May cause dizziness on standing (postural hypotension). Do not crush.

sildenafil.general_guidance: Take 30-60 minutes before sexual activity. Do NOT use with nitrates (dangerous blood pressure drop). Avoid heavy meals before use.
sildenafil.contraindications: Nitrate use, severe heart disease, recent stroke/heart attack, hypotension

clotrimazole.uses: Fungal skin infections, athlete's foot, jock itch, ringworm, oral thrush, vaginal candidiasis
clotrimazole.general_guidance: Apply thin layer to affected area and 2cm around it. Continue for 1-2 weeks after symptoms clear.

fluconazole.uses: Vaginal candidiasis, oral thrush, systemic fungal infections, ringworm
fluconazole.general_guidance: Single 150mg dose for vaginal thrush. Take with or without food.

terbinafine.uses: Nail fungal infections (onychomycosis), ringworm, athlete's foot — very effective for nails
terbinafine.general_guidance: For nail infections: take for 6 weeks (fingernails), 12 weeks (toenails). Continue cream 1-2 weeks after clearing.

acyclovir.uses: Herpes simplex (cold sores, genital herpes), chickenpox (varicella), herpes zoster (shingles)
acyclovir.general_guidance: Start as early as possible. Drink plenty of water to prevent kidney problems. Take with or without food.

hydroxyzine.uses: Anxiety, itching (urticaria, eczema), nausea, pre-operative sedation
hydroxyzine.general_guidance: Causes significant drowsiness — take at bedtime or when not driving.

lactulose.general_guidance: Takes 24-48 hours to work. Can be taken with water, juice or milk. Safe for long-term use.

iron_folate.uses: Iron deficiency anaemia, pregnancy anaemia, folic acid deficiency
iron_folate.general_guidance: Take on empty stomach for best absorption, but take with food if stomach upset. Vitamin C (orange juice) increases absorption. Black stools are normal and harmless.

vitamin_d.uses: Vitamin D deficiency, osteoporosis, bone health, immune support, muscle weakness, depression
vitamin_d.general_guidance: Take with fatty meal (improves absorption). Most Indians are deficient — get levels tested. Weekly/monthly high-dose options available.

vitamin_b12.uses: B12 deficiency, peripheral neuropathy, anaemia, fatigue, vegetarian diet supplementation
vitamin_b12.general_guidance: Take with or without food. Vegetarians and vegans are at high risk of deficiency. Sublingual forms have better absorption.

calcium_vitamin_d.uses: Calcium deficiency, osteoporosis prevention, post-menopausal bone loss, rickets
calcium_vitamin_d.general_guidance: Take with meals for better absorption. Calcium carbonate needs stomach acid. Space doses throughout day (body absorbs max ~500mg at once).

multivitamin.uses: Nutritional deficiencies, general health maintenance, recovery from illness, energy
multivitamin.general_guidance: Take with breakfast. Do not exceed recommended dose. Fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E, K) can accumulate — take as directed.

oral_rehydration.uses: Dehydration from diarrhea, vomiting, fever, heat exhaustion, exercise
oral_rehydration.general_guidance: Dissolve one sachet in 1 litre of clean water. Drink slowly. Make fresh every 24 hours. Do not add extra water or sugar.
oral_rehydration.contraindications: Severe vomiting preventing oral intake (use IV fluids then)

probiotics.uses: Antibiotic-associated diarrhea, IBS, gastroenteritis, restoration of gut flora, bloating
probiotics.general_guidance: Take 2 hours apart from antibiotics. Can be taken with food. Keep refrigerated unless shelf-stable formulation.

cyclopam.uses: Stomach cramps, IBS pain, menstrual cramps, renal colic pain
cyclopam.general_guidance: Take 30 minutes before meals for stomach cramps. May cause dryness in mouth and blurred vision.

mefenamic_acid.uses: Menstrual pain (dysmenorrhoea), mild to moderate pain, dental pain, postoperative pain
mefenamic_acid.general_guidance: Take with food. Start 1-2 days before expected period onset for best effect on menstrual pain.
mefenamic_acid.contraindications: Stomach ulcers, kidney disease, pregnancy, IBD

dolo_650.uses: Fever, headache, body ache, cold & flu — most commonly used medicine in India for COVID-19 fever
dolo_650.general_guidance: Most popular fever tablet in India. Do not combine with other paracetamol products. Max 4 doses/day. Take with water.

crocin.uses: Fever, headache, body ache, cold & flu symptoms (Crocin Cold variants)
crocin.general_guidance: Widely available OTC. Follow dosage on pack. Do not double-dose if already taking another paracetamol product.

norflox_tz.uses: Infectious diarrhea, gastroenteritis, traveller's diarrhea, amoebic dysentery — very commonly prescribed in India for gut infections
norflox_tz.general_guidance: Take with food. Complete course. AVOID ALCOHOL completely (tinidazole reaction). Prescription required.

stugeron.uses: Vertigo, dizziness, motion sickness, Meniere's disease, tinnitus
stugeron.general_guidance: Take with food. Causes drowsiness — avoid driving. Very effective for vertigo.

vertin.uses: Meniere's disease, vertigo, dizziness, tinnitus, balance disorders
vertin.general_guidance: Take with food. Usually given 3 times daily. Long-term treatment may be needed for Meniere's.

codeine.uses: Severe cough, mild to moderate pain
codeine.general_guidance: PRESCRIPTION ONLY. May cause drowsiness and constipation. Addiction risk — use minimum effective dose.
codeine.contraindications: Children under 12, respiratory depression, asthma, liver disease

minoxidil.uses: Male pattern baldness (androgenic alopecia), female hair loss
minoxidil.general_guidance: Apply to dry scalp twice daily. Results in 3-6 months. Must continue use — hair loss returns when stopped.

clarithromycin.uses: Respiratory infections, sinusitis, skin infections, H. pylori, community-acquired pneumonia
clarithromycin.general_guidance: Take with or without food. Complete the full course. Avoid grapefruit juice.
clarithromycin.contraindications: Liver disease, heart rhythm problems (QT prolongation), certain drug interactions

isoxsuprine.uses: Premature labour (tocolytic), peripheral vascular disease, threatened abortion
isoxsuprine.general_guidance: Take as prescribed. Used in obstetrics to relax uterine muscles. Do not stop without doctor advice.
isoxsuprine.contraindications: Active bleeding, immediately post-partum, heart disease

abciximab.uses: Prevention of blood clots during coronary procedures, unstable angina
abciximab.general_guidance: Hospital use only — administered by injection under medical supervision.
abciximab.contraindications: Active bleeding, recent surgery, severe hypertension

doxylamine_pyridoxine.uses: Nausea and vomiting in pregnancy (morning sickness), general nausea
doxylamine_pyridoxine.general_guidance: Take at bedtime as directed. May cause drowsiness. Do not drive after taking.

combiflam.uses: Pain relief, fever, headache, dental pain, menstrual cramps, muscle pain — dual action combination
combiflam.general_guidance: Always take with food. Very effective for pain. Do not use on empty stomach.

pan_d.uses: Acidity with nausea, GERD, bloating, gastroparesis — very commonly prescribed in India
pan_d.general_guidance: Take 30 minutes before breakfast. One of the most prescribed combinations for acidity + nausea in India.

cheston_cold.uses: Common cold, runny nose, sneezing, fever, congestion — all-in-one cold tablet
cheston_cold.general_guidance: Causes drowsiness — avoid driving. Take at bedtime preferably. Do not take with other antihistamines.

benadryl.uses: Cough (especially wet cough with mucus), allergic cough, cold-related cough
benadryl.general_guidance: Causes significant drowsiness. Do not drive. Avoid in children under 2. Do not exceed recommended dose.
benadryl.contraindications: Glaucoma, prostate enlargement, asthma (dry cough variant), children under 2

ascoril.uses: Wet cough with mucus, bronchitis, chest congestion, COPD — helps loosen and expel mucus
ascoril.general_guidance: Shake well before use. Take with water. Commonly prescribed for productive cough in India.

meftal_spas.uses: Menstrual cramps (dysmenorrhoea), stomach spasms, IBS pain — very commonly used by women in India
meftal_spas.general_guidance: Take with food. Start 1-2 days before expected period for best effect on cramps.
meftal_spas.contraindications: Stomach ulcers, kidney disease, pregnancy, glaucoma

allegra_120.uses: Allergic rhinitis, hives, seasonal allergies, dust allergy, skin allergies — does NOT cause drowsiness
allegra_120.general_guidance: Best antihistamine for daytime use — non-drowsy. Take once daily. Do not take with grapefruit juice.

montair_lc.uses: Allergic rhinitis, chronic urticaria, asthma with allergy — very popular combination in India
montair_lc.general_guidance: Take at night. One of the most prescribed allergy combinations in India.

taxim_o.uses: UTI, typhoid, tonsillitis, pneumonia, gonorrhoea — widely prescribed antibiotic in India
taxim_o.general_guidance: Take with food to reduce stomach upset. Complete full course. Prescription required.

augmentin_625.uses: Sinusitis, tonsillitis, chest infections, skin infections, UTI, dental abscess — broad spectrum antibiotic
augmentin_625.general_guidance: Take with meals. Complete the full course. Very commonly prescribed in India.

zerodol_p.uses: Joint pain, arthritis, back pain, dental pain, post-operative pain — commonly prescribed pain combination in India
zerodol_p.general_guidance: Take with food. Very effective for musculoskeletal pain. Do not use long-term without doctor advice.

aceclofenac.uses: Arthritis, back pain, joint pain, dental pain, sports injuries — popular NSAID in India
aceclofenac.general_guidance: Take with food. Better gastric safety than Diclofenac. Common pain killer in India.
aceclofenac.contraindications: Stomach ulcers, kidney disease, heart failure

p_500.uses: Mild fever, headache, mild pain — standard 500mg paracetamol
p_500.general_guidance: OTC. Standard adult dose. Do not exceed 4g/day. Safe for most people.

voveran_gel.uses: Local pain relief for joint pain, sports injuries, back pain, muscle strains — applied directly to skin
voveran_gel.general_guidance: Apply thin layer to affected area 3-4 times daily. Do not apply on broken skin. Wash hands after.
voveran_gel.contraindications: Open wounds, eczema on application area

moov.uses: Muscle pain, joint pain, sprains, back pain — applied externally for fast local relief
moov.general_guidance: Apply and massage gently. Do not use on open wounds. Keep away from eyes. Very popular OTC pain relief in India.

digene.uses: Immediate relief from acidity, heartburn, gas, bloating, sour stomach — most popular antacid in India
digene.general_guidance: Chew or take as liquid after meals and at bedtime. Provides instant relief. Not for long-term use.

pudin_hara.uses: Gas, bloating, indigestion, nausea, stomach discomfort — traditional Indian digestive remedy
pudin_hara.general_guidance: Take after meals. Safe for most ages. Very popular OTC digestive in India.

liv_52.uses: Liver protection, fatty liver, loss of appetite, post-alcohol liver support, hepatitis support
liv_52.general_guidance: Ayurvedic liver tonic. Take with meals. Long-term safe use. Very popular OTC supplement in India.
liv_52.contraindications: No major contraindications. Consult doctor for serious liver disease.

revital.uses: General health maintenance, fatigue, weakness, nutritional gaps, post-illness recovery, energy
revital.general_guidance: Take with breakfast. One of India's most popular health supplements. Do not exceed recommended dose.

ors_electral.uses: Dehydration from diarrhea, vomiting, fever, heat, exercise — most important OTC medicine in India
ors_electral.general_guidance: Mix 1 sachet in 1 litre clean water. Drink slowly. Make fresh every 24 hours. Essential for diarrhea management.
ors_electral.contraindications: Severe vomiting preventing oral intake (use IV then)

d_cold.uses: Common cold, nasal congestion, runny nose, sneezing, fever — classic Indian cold medicine
d_cold.general_guidance: Causes drowsiness. Take at night. One of India's oldest cold remedies.

pantop_d.uses: Acidity with bloating/nausea, GERD, reflux disease — one of the top 10 prescribed medicines in India
pantop_d.general_guidance: Take 30 min before breakfast. Very effective combination for post-meal acidity and bloating.

sporlac.uses: Antibiotic-associated diarrhea, IBS, restoring gut flora, bloating, digestive issues
sporlac.general_guidance: Take 2 hours away from antibiotics. Can take with food. Safe for all ages.

zincovit.uses: Zinc deficiency, immunity boosting, wound healing, hair loss, COVID recovery, skin health
zincovit.general_guidance: Take with meals. Zinc is one of the most common deficiencies in India. Important for immunity.

telekast_l.uses: Allergic rhinitis, chronic urticaria, asthma with allergies — widely prescribed combination in India
telekast_l.general_guidance: Take at night. Effective for both nasal and skin allergies.

rantac.uses: Acidity, GERD, peptic ulcer, heartburn — older but widely used antacid in India
rantac.general_guidance: Take before meals or at bedtime. Less powerful than PPIs but faster acting for symptom relief.

avomine.uses: Motion sickness, nausea, vomiting, allergies, travel sickness — very popular before travel in India
avomine.general_guidance: Take 1-2 hours before travel. Causes significant drowsiness — do not drive. Very effective for motion sickness.

lopamide.uses: Acute diarrhea, traveller's diarrhea, IBS-related diarrhea — stops loose motions quickly
lopamide.general_guidance: Take after each loose stool. Do not exceed 8mg/day. Do not use if blood in stool or high fever.
lopamide.contraindications: Bacterial diarrhea with fever, blood in stool, children under 2

sorbiline.uses: Constipation, hard stools, straining during defecation — gentle laxative for regular use
sorbiline.general_guidance: Take at night with water. Works within 24-48 hours. Drink plenty of water. Safe for long-term use.

hair4u.uses: Male pattern baldness, hair loss (alopecia) — topical treatment applied to scalp
hair4u.general_guidance: Apply to dry scalp twice daily. Results visible in 3-6 months. Must continue — hair loss returns if stopped.

candid_b.uses: Fungal skin infections with inflammation, ringworm with itching, athlete's foot, jock itch
candid_b.general_guidance: Apply thin layer twice daily. Use for 2-4 weeks. Do not use on face. Do not use long-term — steroid side effects.
candid_b.contraindications: Face application, broken skin, long-term use

soframycin.uses: Minor cuts, wounds, burns, skin infections — prevents infection in minor injuries
soframycin.general_guidance: Clean wound first. Apply thin layer and cover. Very popular OTC antibiotic cream in India.
soframycin.contraindications: Large wounds, deep burns (use prescription antibiotics)

boroline.uses: Dry cracked skin, chapped lips, minor skin irritations, antiseptic for minor cuts — iconic Indian OTC cream
boroline.general_guidance: Apply to clean skin. Safe for daily use. Iconic Indian household medicine since decades.

betnesol.uses: Severe allergic reactions, inflammatory conditions, skin diseases, asthma, autoimmune conditions
betnesol.general_guidance: Prescription only. Take with food. Do not stop abruptly. Short-term use recommended.
betnesol.contraindications: Active infections, diabetes, osteoporosis, stomach ulcers

glycomet_gp.uses: Type 2 Diabetes — dual action combination widely prescribed in India
glycomet_gp.general_guidance: Take with breakfast. Monitor blood sugar. Do not skip doses. Most common diabetes combination in India.
glycomet_gp.contraindications: Kidney disease, liver disease, Type 1 diabetes

januvia.uses: Type 2 Diabetes — newer class of diabetes medicine, fewer side effects than older drugs
januvia.general_guidance: Take with or without food. Low risk of hypoglycemia. Prescription required.

telma_am.uses: Hypertension (high blood pressure) — very popular combination for BP in India
telma_am.general_guidance: Take once daily. One of the most prescribed BP combinations in India. Do not stop without doctor advice.

ecosprin_av.uses: Prevention of heart attack and stroke, post-angioplasty care, high cardiovascular risk — very common cardiac combination
ecosprin_av.general_guidance: Take after dinner. Lifelong medication for most heart patients. Do not stop without cardiologist advice.
ecosprin_av.contraindications: Active bleeding, peptic ulcer, dengue fever

telma_h.uses: Hypertension — BP medicine with diuretic for better control
telma_h.general_guidance: Take in the morning. May increase urination. Monitor potassium and kidney function.

azithral_500.uses: Respiratory infections, typhoid, COVID-19 treatment protocols, skin infections, STIs — most popular antibiotic in India
azithral_500.general_guidance: Usually 3-5 day course. Take on empty stomach or with food. Very commonly prescribed in India.

calpol_500.uses: Fever and pain in children and adults — trusted brand for paediatric fever in India
calpol_500.general_guidance: Safe for children. Dose based on weight for children. Widely available OTC.

spasmo_proxyvon.uses: Severe abdominal cramps, renal colic, biliary colic — strong pain and spasm relief
spasmo_proxyvon.general_guidance: Prescription required. May cause drowsiness. Do not drive. Tramadol component — do not use long-term.

volini.uses: Muscle pain, joint pain, sports injuries, back pain, neck pain — applied directly on pain area
volini.general_guidance: Spray or apply on affected area. Massage gently. Very popular OTC pain spray in India.

otrivin.uses: Blocked/stuffy nose from cold, sinusitis, allergic rhinitis — provides instant nasal relief
otrivin.general_guidance: Do NOT use for more than 3-5 days — causes rebound congestion. 1-2 sprays per nostril. Very effective short-term.
otrivin.contraindications: Hypertension, hyperthyroidism, do not use more than 5 days

o2.uses: UTI, respiratory infections, skin infections, conjunctivitis (eye drops), ear infections
o2.general_guidance: Prescription required. Take on empty stomach. Common alternative to Ciprofloxacin.
o2.contraindications: Children under 18 (except eye/ear drops), pregnancy, epilepsy

acutret.uses: Severe nodular/cystic acne, acne that hasn't responded to other treatments — most powerful acne medicine
acutret.general_guidance: Take with fatty meal for best absorption. Causes extreme dryness of skin, lips, eyes. Must avoid pregnancy — use contraception. Monthly blood tests required. PRESCRIPTION ONLY.
acutret.contraindications: Pregnancy (severe birth defects), breastfeeding, liver disease, high triglycerides

momate.uses: Skin inflammation, eczema, psoriasis, dermatitis, dark patches (melasma when combined), itching
momate.general_guidance: Apply thin layer once daily. Do not use on face for long periods. Prescription required. Do not use more than 2 weeks on sensitive skin.
momate.contraindications: Infected skin, rosacea, acne, prolonged face use

cligel_a.uses: Acne vulgaris (pimples), blackheads, whiteheads — dual action kills bacteria and unclogs pores
cligel_a.general_guidance: Apply pea-sized amount on clean dry face at night. Start 2-3 times/week, then daily. Avoid sun exposure. Use sunscreen in morning.

eberfine_m.uses: Fungal infections of skin and nose area, tinea, candidiasis, ringworm
eberfine_m.general_guidance: Apply thin layer to affected area. Complete full course even if symptoms improve.

vitamin_c_capsule.uses: Vitamin C deficiency, immunity boosting, skin health, collagen synthesis, wound healing, antioxidant
vitamin_c_capsule.general_guidance: Take after food. Water-soluble — excess is excreted safely. Commonly prescribed for skin health and immunity.

mupact.uses: Bacterial skin infections, impetigo, infected wounds, nasal MRSA decolonisation — very effective topical antibiotic
mupact.general_guidance: Apply thin layer 3 times daily for 5-10 days. Do not use inside nose for more than 5 days. Do not use long-term.

triclear_foam.generic: Salicylic Acid + Glycolic Acid Foaming Cleanser
triclear_foam.uses: Acne-prone skin cleansing, removes excess oil, unclogs pores, reduces blackheads
triclear_foam.general_guidance: Use as face wash morning and night. Rinse thoroughly. Follow with moisturiser. Avoid contact with eyes.
triclear_foam.contraindications: Dry or sensitive skin (may worsen dryness)

scarend_silicone.uses: Reducing acne scars, surgical scars, keloids, hypertrophic scars — flattens and fades scars
scarend_silicone.general_guidance: Apply thin layer on clean scar twice daily. Massage gently. Use consistently for 3-6 months for best results.

uv_doux.uses: Sun protection, prevents UV damage, prevents tanning, post-acne skin protection, prevents dark spots
uv_doux.general_guidance: Apply generously 20 minutes before sun exposure. Reapply every 2-3 hours outdoors. Essential after acne treatment.

skinoperf.uses: Skin brightening, reducing dark spots, open pores, oily skin control, post-acne marks
skinoperf.general_guidance: Apply few drops to clean face at night. Use before moisturiser. Give 6-8 weeks to see results.

acne_lotion.uses: Acne vulgaris, pimples, blackheads — kills acne-causing bacteria (P. acnes)
acne_lotion.general_guidance: Apply to affected area after cleansing. Use morning or night as directed. Do not apply to broken skin.

hydronic_moisturizer.uses: Dry skin, post-treatment skin hydration, barrier repair, skin softening after acne/retinoid treatment
hydronic_moisturizer.general_guidance: Apply at night on clean face. Use after serums. Helps repair skin barrier damaged by acne treatments like Acutret.

acne_lode.uses: Acne vulgaris, pimples, whiteheads, comedones — kills P. acnes bacteria on skin surface
acne_lode.general_guidance: Apply to clean face at night on affected areas. Use consistently. Avoid near eyes and mouth.
